CHANGE RECORD 
 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID  is updated and a row is 
added to this table.  The third column specifies which sections or sub -sections 
have been updated.  The fourth column should mention the version of the 
product to which the change applies.

I INTRODUCTION 
 
I.1 Product name  
OMI_CLIMATE_SST_IST_ARCTIC_area_averaged_anomalies,  
OMI_CLIMATE_SST_IST_ARCTIC_trend and 
OMI_CLIMATE_SST_IST_ARCTIC_anomaly 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
All anomalies have been calculated with respect to a monthly climatology from 
1991-2020 (30 years), defined according to WMO recommendations (WMO, 
2017) and the most recent practice from the U.S. National Oceanic and 
Atmospheric Administration practice (ht tps://wmo.int/media/news/updated-
30-year-reference-period-reflects-changing-climate). To calculate the trends in 
the surface temperature time series and the confidence intervals on the trends, 
we used the Mann-Kendall Tau-b non-parametric function with Sens method to 
compute a coefficient representing a trend for equally spaced data (Mann 
1945; Sen 1968; Kendall 1975). A 95% confidence interval for the trend estimate 
is chosen here.  
The Sea Surface and Ice Surface Temperature Level 4 analysis product that 
provides the input to the monthly averages is taken from the multi -year, 
reprocessed dataset SEAICE_ARC_PHY_CLIMATE_L4_MY_011_016 with the 
regular updates based upon C3S observations.
